articles.title: Spillover Effect Dead Ends: SC’s Flipkart Verdict, Quashed AO Orders & Dawn of Income Tax Act 2025
articles.summary: The Supreme Court set aside an NCLAT order that relied on a non-existent income tax quasi-judicial order in a case involving Flipkart. The matter must now be reconsidered from scratch based on established competition law principles. This ruling has implications for tax and regulatory enforcement in India. The decision underscores the need for adherence to legal procedures in tax-related disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court set aside the NCLAT order for relying on a non-existent quasi-judicial income tax order. The key takeaway is that the matter must be reconsidered afresh following settled competition law principles. The post Spillover Effect Dead Ends: SC’s Flipkart Verdict, Quashed AO Orders   Dawn of Income Tax Act 2025 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: States are not Centre’s subordinates: Justice B.V. Nagarathna
articles.summary: Justice B.V. Nagarathna stated that the Centre should treat States as equals under the Constitution, emphasizing federalism as a system of co-equals rather than hierarchical subordinates. She highlighted that the Centre ought to view States as coordinates in a federal arrangement. This statement underscores the importance of cooperative federalism in India's governance structure.
articles.raw_summary: Justice Nagarathna says the Centre ought to view the States as coordinates and federalism as a constitutional arrangement of co-equals

articles.title: Power to Grant Tax Exemption includes Power to Withdraw/Modify it in Public Interest; Beneficiary has No Vested Right: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the government has the power to withdraw or modify tax exemptions in the public interest, and beneficiaries do not have a legally enforceable right to such concessions. The court stated that the right to enjoy a concession is conditional and can be revoked. The ruling clarifies the government's authority over fiscal policies and tax exemptions.
articles.raw_summary: The recipient of a concession has no legally enforceable right against the Government to grant of a concession except to enjoy the benefits of the concession during the period of its grant. This right to enjoy is a defeasible one.  The post Power to Grant Tax Exemption includes Power to Withdraw/Modify it in Public Interest; Beneficiary has No Vested Right: Supreme Court appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: Entire Bank Deposits Can’t Be Taxed U/s 69A – Only Reasonable Commission Income Taxable
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that only commission income earned by a bank agent can be taxed as unexplained income under Section 69A, not the entire bank deposits. The tribunal upheld an 8% estimation of commission income as reasonable. This decision clarifies tax liability for commission agents and impacts how unexplained income is assessed under tax laws.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether full bank deposits of a commission agent can be taxed as unexplained income. The ruling held only commission income taxable, with 8% estimation upheld as reasonable. articles.title: ‘Bad in law’: National Green Tribunal stays Assam order to deploy forest officers for poll duty
articles.summary: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has stayed an Assam government order that directed forest officers to be deployed for election duty. The petitioner argued that the order violated the 2002 Biological Diversity Act and a 2024 Supreme Court ruling prohibiting the use of forest staff for election purposes. The NGT's decision highlights conflicts between electoral processes and environmental governance, raising questions about the legal boundaries of government orders.
articles.raw_summary: The petitioner argued that the order violated the 2002 Biological Diversity Act and a 2024 Supreme Court order against deploying forest staffers for elections.

articles.title: FEMA SCN Set Aside – SC Holds Adjudication Cannot Override Competent Authority Findings Pending Appeal
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that adjudication proceedings under FEMA cannot override findings by the Competent Authority if no basis for seizure exists. The Court held that such proceedings lack jurisdictional facts and must be set aside, especially when an appeal is pending. This decision impacts how enforcement actions under FEMA are conducted, ensuring they align with the Competent Authority's findings. The ruling underscores the importance of jurisdictional integrity in regulatory enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that where the Competent Authority finds no basis for seizure, the foundation of the SCN collapses. It ruled that proceedings cannot continue without jurisdictional facts. articles.title: Section 80P Deduction Allowed on Bank Interest – ITAT Follows Tumkur Merchants, Distinguishes Totagars
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that interest earned on bank deposits made from surplus business funds is considered business income, not income from other sources. This allows businesses to claim tax deductions under Section 80P. The ruling follows a previous decision in Tumkur Merchants' case and distinguishes it from the Totagars case. The decision impacts how businesses classify and tax their interest income.
articles.raw_summary: The ruling holds that deposits made from surplus business funds are part of operational activity. Interest earned on such deposits is business income and not income from other sources. Therefore, Section 80P benefit was granted. The post Section 80P Deduction Allowed on Bank Interest   ITAT Follows Tumkur Merchants, Distinguishes Totagars appeared first on TaxGuru .

The policy aims to standardize taxation for EVs while generating revenue for the government.

articles.summary: The article explains that under the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime, tax applies to Transferable Development Rights (TDR), construction, and sale of units in Joint Development Agreements (JDAs) for residential projects. Tax is primarily payable on unsold units at project completion, ensuring no double taxation. The clarification aims to resolve ambiguities in tax compliance for developers and buyers under JDAs.

articles.title: No 143(2) Notice, No Reassessment: ITAT Quashes Proceedings Despite Delayed ITR
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) quashed income tax reassessment proceedings against a taxpayer because the tax department failed to issue a mandatory notice under Section 143(2) of the Income Tax Act. The ruling highlights that the absence of such a notice renders the entire assessment process invalid, setting a precedent for future tax disputes. The case involved a delayed income tax return (ITR) filing, but the tribunal prioritized procedural compliance over the delay.

This ruling impacts how tax deductions are processed and could influence future tax policy interpretations. The decision underscores the importance of factual substantiation in tax claims.

articles.title: First GST Appellate Authority cannot remand matters to Adjudicating Authority: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that the First GST Appellate Authority cannot remand cases back to the Adjudicating Authority, as it violates Section 107(11) of the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act. The court emphasized that appellate authorities must decide cases themselves rather than sending them back for reconsideration. This ruling clarifies the powers of GST appellate bodies and ensures procedural fairness in tax disputes. The decision directly impacts how GST appeals are handled,

articles.title: GST Section 74 Not for System Mismatches: What Sterling & Wilson Case Means for Taxpayers
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that mismatches in tax filings under GST Section 74 do not automatically imply fraudulent intent. The case involving Sterling & Wilson highlights that penal provisions require evidence of deliberate tax evasion. This ruling clarifies the threshold for invoking penalties under GST, impacting taxpayers and tax administration. The decision underscores the need for intent to evade tax, not just procedural errors, in tax disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that mismatch alone does not prove fraud under Section 74. The key takeaway is that penal provisions cannot be invoked without evidence of intent to evade tax. articles.summary: The rate of interest on Government of India Floating Rate Bond 2028 (FRB 2028) applicable for the half year April 04, 2026 to October 03, 2026 shall be 6.17 per cent per annum. articles.summary: The Karnataka Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that bank rebates received on corporate card usage do not qualify as a 'supply' under GST law. Since no consideration or quid pro quo is involved, such rebates are treated as money and are not taxable. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of financial transactions between banks and corporate entities.
articles.raw_summary: The Authority held that rebates received from a bank on corporate card usage do not constitute a supply under GST law. Since there is no quid pro quo or consideration, such transactions are treated as money and are not taxable. articles.title: Legal Research, Understanding Impugned Order No Ground To Condone Delay: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has held that conducting legal research or understanding the impugned order cannot be a ground for condoning the delay. Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma said that if such a ground were to be accepted as a sufficient explanation, it would render the law of limitation and the principles of delay and laches largely otiose. articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that transaction value cannot be rejected without proper inquiry. The decision states that using NIDB data alone to enhance valuation is invalid because it lacks investigation and due process. This ruling impacts tax assessment procedures in India, emphasizing the importance of thorough verification before altering declared transaction values.

The decision emphasizes that independent verification is required for such demands to be valid. This ruling impacts how tax authorities assess and enforce Service Tax compliance in India.

articles.title: Client Liable for CA Fees: Bombay HC Confirms Cheque Bounce Case After 9 Years
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court upheld a conviction in a cheque bounce case after nine years, confirming that the accused could not disprove liability under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act. The court found the complainant's evidence sufficient and the presumption of liability unrebutted. The case involved a dispute over fees charged by a chartered accountant, with the court ruling in favor of the complainant.

The court upheld conviction as the presumption remained unrebutted and evidence supported the complainant’s claim. articles.summary: The court overturned GST orders issued against a taxpayer for wrongly availing Input Tax Credit (ITC), ruling that the proceedings were ex-parte and violated the taxpayer's right to respond to a show cause notice before adjudication. The original and appellate authorities had not provided the taxpayer an opportunity to reply, leading to the orders being set aside.

articles.title: Draft IT amendments risk over-censorship, over-compliance, say experts
articles.summary: Experts warn that India's draft IT Rules amendments could lead to over-censorship and over-compliance. The rules risk impacting free speech, surveillance practices, and digital platform operations in India.

articles.title: Plea In Supreme Court Challenges Transgender Persons Amendment Act 2026, Says Omitting Self-Identification Violates Art 21
articles.summary: A petition has been filed in the Supreme Court challenging the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act, 2026. The petitioners argue that the law violates the right to self-identification of gender, as established by the 2014 NALSA judgment, and infringes upon Article 21 of the Constitution. The case highlights concerns over the omission of self-identification provisions in the amended law.
articles.raw_summary: The petitioners argued that the right to self-identification of gender conferred by the 2014 NALSA judgment cannot be taken away by a statute.

articles.title: ITC Denied on Leasehold Rights as Linked to Construction of Immovable Property: AAAR Tamilnadu
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAAR) ruled that an industrial plant installed for long-term use and not marketable as a whole is considered immovable property. As a result, ITC on related inputs was denied to the company involved. The decision hinges on the classification of the plant under tax laws, affecting how businesses can claim tax credits for such assets.
articles.raw_summary: The authority analysed whether an industrial plant is movable or immovable. It held that the plant, installed for long-term use and not marketable as a whole, is immovable property. Consequently, ITC on related inputs was denied. articles.title: Real GDP growth may slow on costly crude: Credit rating agencies
articles.summary: Credit rating agencies Icra, Care Ratings, and Crisil have projected India's GDP growth at around 6.5% in FY27, citing high crude oil prices as a key factor. Icra assumes an average crude price above $85 per barrel, while Care Ratings uses $100 per barrel. Crisil notes that strong corporate balance sheets may mitigate credit risks despite these challenges.
articles.raw_summary: Icra projected GDP growth at 6.5% in FY27, assuming average crude price remains in excess of $85 a barrel this year, while Care Ratings projected a similar growth on higher average fuel-price assumptions-at $100 per barrel. However, Crisil expects robust balance sheet structures to provide sufficient undergirding against credit risks for an overwhelming percentage of industries it covers.

articles.title: Addition Under Section 40A(2)(a) Deleted Due to Incorrect Comparison of Services
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that the Assessing Officer incorrectly compared dissimilar email marketing services to determine excess payments under Section 40A(2)(a) of the Income Tax Act. The tribunal clarified that only comparable services can be used for arm’s length evaluations, leading to the deletion of the tax addition. This ruling impacts how tax authorities assess cross-border transactions and transfer pricing for services.

Data localization rules and high tariffs on agriculture products are also cited. The US is pushing for more access to India's dairy market. Discussions for an interim trade agreement are ongoing.
articles.raw_summary: America has identified several trade barriers in India. These include India's preference for local satellites for DTH services and the exclusion of US payment firms from UPI. Data localization rules and high tariffs on agriculture products are also cited. The US is pushing for more access to India's dairy market. Discussions for an interim trade agreement are ongoing.

articles.title: Govt plans rare earth magnet push; pre-bid meeting set for 7 April
articles.summary: The Indian government is advancing a scheme to promote rare earth magnets, with a pre-bid meeting scheduled for 7 April. The meeting will clarify guidelines, eligibility, and implementation details for domestic and international industry players. This initiative aims to boost domestic production and reduce reliance on imports. The scheme falls under the government's broader push for self-reliance in critical technologies.
articles.raw_summary: The pre-bid meeting will provide the stakeholders, including domestic and international industry players and prospective bidders, an opportunity to gain a clear understanding of the scheme’s guidelines, eligibility criteria and implementation framework.

articles.title: One battle after another: How India fared in the year of Trump tariffs
articles.summary: Beginning in April 2025, India found itself navigating turbulent waters due to sweeping changes in US trade policy led by President Trump. The imposition of tariffs posed a substantial threat to Indian exports, prompting New Delhi to innovate through strategic diversification and robust domestic reforms.

articles.summary: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri represented India on Thursday at a UK-hosted summit of nearly 30 nations that focused on ensuring safe shipping through the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran's partial blockade of the strategic waterway which has severely disrupted global oil and gas supplies.

articles.title: Crude sustaining above $100 will push inflation beyond 6%, trigger rate hikes: HSBC
articles.summary: HSBC economists warn that crude oil prices staying above $100 per barrel could push India's inflation beyond 6%, prompting the central bank to raise interest rates. The article suggests policymakers must balance economic growth with inflation control, noting that early demand stimulation risks worsening inflation. It also mentions that raising fuel prices could help manage the fiscal deficit. The analysis emphasizes the need for a neutral approach to monetary and fiscal policies.
articles.raw_summary: Crude oil above one hundred dollars a barrel will push inflation past six percent. This could prompt interest rate hikes. Economists suggest a neutral approach for monetary and fiscal policies. Stimulating demand too early risks high inflation. Policymakers must balance growth and inflation control. Raising fuel prices can help manage the fiscal deficit.

articles.title: The economic logic of sin taxes: Why they are a form of fiscal morality
articles.summary: The article explores the concept of 'sin taxes'—taxes on harmful products like tobacco or alcohol—and argues they should address the social costs these products impose on society. Former CBIC chairman Najib Shah notes that revenue from such taxes should be used to mitigate these harms. The discussion highlights the intersection of fiscal policy and public health, though it does not reference specific Indian regulations or actions.
articles.raw_summary: What is often overlooked in ‘sin’ taxation is that it is fundamentally meant to address the social costs imposed on society by the consumption of harmful products. It therefore stands to reason that the revenue generated from such taxation should be used to mitigate these harms, notes former CBIC chairman Najib Shah.

articles.title: LPG shortage hits MSMEs, disrupts production: Industry voices
articles.summary: LPG shortages are disrupting production at micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) across India, forcing units to scale down operations and absorb higher fuel costs. Industry leaders like KarthiKeyan Mounaguruswamy (CODISSIA President) and Sunil Chordia (CII National, MSME Council) warn of cascading production cuts due to limited alternatives. The crisis is straining profitability and output, with no immediate relief in sight. The article underscores the urgent need for policy intervention
articles.raw_summary: KarthiKeyan Mounaguruswamy, President of Coimbatore District Small Industries Association (CODISSIA) and Sunil Chordia, Chairman CII National, MSME Council & CMD at Rajratan Global Wire highlight how LPG supply disruptions are triggering cascading production cuts across MSMEs, forcing units to scale down operations and absorb higher fuel costs, with limited alternatives and mounting pressure on profitability and output.

This could impact global semiconductor supply chains and India's technology and trade policies.

articles.title: More LNG supply for urea plants to boost production: Government
articles.summary: The Indian government will boost LNG gas supply to urea plants from Monday, raising it to 90% of their average consumption. This move aims to enhance urea production for upcoming crop seasons. Additionally, gas supply to other industrial and commercial sectors will increase by 10%, based on available inventory and scheduled LNG arrivals.

This move, considering available inventory and scheduled LNG arrivals, will also enhance gas supply to other industrial and commercial sectors by 10%.

articles.summary: Ashwini Bhide, a 1995-batch IAS officer currently serving as Additional Chief Secretary in the Chief Minister’s Office, has been appointed as the first woman BMC Commissioner. This appointment is significant as it marks a milestone for women in leadership roles within municipal governance and may influence urban policy implementation in Mumbai.

articles.title: US tightens chip export controls, curb China's tech rise
articles.summary: The US has tightened export controls on semiconductor manufacturing equipment to curb China's technological advancement. A committee stated that semiconductors are central to global tech competition and introduced the MATCH Act to strengthen these controls and coordinate a global approach. The move aims to restrict China's access to advanced chipmaking tools, which could impact global supply chains and India's tech industry.
articles.raw_summary: The committee further stated that semiconductor manufacturing lies at the core of the technological competition with China, adding that the MATCH Act is designed to strengthen export controls and create a more coordinated global approach, as highlighted in its release.

articles.title: De-dollarisation, war, and debt: Why gold is regaining monetary relevance
articles.summary: Gold is gaining importance globally due to rising geopolitical tensions, high debt levels, and efforts to reduce reliance on the US dollar. Central banks, including those in emerging economies, are increasing gold purchases, reflecting concerns over fiat currency stability. This shift could impact India's foreign exchange reserves, trade policies, and monetary strategies as the global financial system moves toward a multipolar order.
articles.raw_summary: Gold is regaining prominence as global markets face geopolitical tensions, rising debt and de-dollarisation trends. Central bank buying, structural shifts in the financial system and weakening confidence in fiat currencies are driving demand. Despite short-term volatility, gold’s long-term outlook remains strong amid a transition towards a multipolar global economic order.

articles.title: Rupee at 100 will be a harsh check on India’s ambitions
articles.summary: India's rupee has hit record lows, exacerbated by Middle East conflict and domestic economic fragilities. The Reserve Bank of India's currency controls backfired, signaling a loss of confidence. A rupee at 100 threatens to increase living costs, strain government finances, and impact aspirational spending, posing a harsh check on the nation's ambitions.

articles.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: A ship owned by a major French company has passed through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil transit route, for the first time since the conflict in the region began. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital chokepoint for global oil shipments, and its stability is crucial for India's energy imports. The event highlights ongoing geopolitical tensions and their potential impact on maritime security and oil supply chains.
articles.raw_summary: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

articles.title: Global leaders work to ease oil price surge as Trump signals more weeks of Iran war
articles.summary: Global leaders in New York and London are attempting to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route, amid ongoing military strikes between the U.S., Israel, and Iran. President Trump has indicated the conflict could persist for two to three more weeks. The surge in oil prices and regional instability may impact India's energy imports and economic policies, given its heavy reliance on crude oil from the Middle East.
articles.raw_summary: In New York and London, European and Middle Eastern countries tried to develop a plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which has largely been closed since the war in Iran began. Following President Trump's address predicting two to three more weeks of war, the U.S. and Israel continued to bomb Iran, and Iran continued its strikes on Gulf countries and Israel. Nick Schifrin reports.

articles.title: On global tensions and India’s economy
articles.summary: In an economy that imports more than four-fifths of its crude oil, external shocks transmitted through energy prices, shipping route, and volatile commodity markets can significantly reshape fiscal arithmetic. articles.title: The architecture of resilience: Why India’s private credit stands apart
articles.summary: India’s private credit market stands resilient amid global stress due to its conservative fund structures, strict leverage norms, and closed-ended AIF framework. Unlike global peers facing liquidity and redemption pressures, India’s system aligns investor timelines with asset maturity, reducing systemic risks while offering significant growth headroom in an underpenetrated market.

articles.title: US Allies Work On Plan B For Hormuz Strait If Trump Walks Away

The move signals deep concern over the waterway's crisis, triggered by US-Iran tensions under President Trump's policies. India, heavily reliant on oil imports, may face indirect consequences from such geopolitical developments.

articles.summary: The Securities Transaction Tax (STT) on equity futures and options has been increased by SEBI, effective immediately. The STT on equity options premium rose from 0.1% to 0.15%, on equity futures from 0.02% to 0.05%, and on equity options exercise from 0.125% to 0.15%. This change aims to adjust tax rates on derivative trading, impacting investors and market participants.

articles.title: World food price rise set to continue if Iran war lasts, FAO says

The situation may prompt India to reassess its agricultural policies or trade strategies to mitigate food inflation.
articles.raw_summary: The FAO Food Price Index, which measures changes in a basket of globally traded food commodities, rose by 2.4% from its revised February level. It is 1% above its value a year ago, although nearly 20% below its March 2022 peak, reached after the start of the war in Ukraine.

articles.title: Informal sector steps up coal stocking over price concerns
articles.summary: Energy-intensive units in India's informal sector are increasing coal stockpiles due to rising costs of imported coal, despite limited storage capacity. This trend suggests market expectations of further price hikes. The situation highlights potential vulnerabilities in India's energy supply chain and may prompt policy responses to stabilize prices or boost domestic production.

articles.title: Pakistan to repay $2 billion loan to UAE amid Middle East tensions
articles.summary: Pakistan is preparing to repay a $2 billion loan to the UAE by the end of the month, originally provided for balance of payment support. The repayment is reportedly linked to recent geopolitical shifts in the Middle East. While Pakistan can meet this obligation, it may need additional external financing for future fiscal needs. The State Bank of Pakistan holds the funds.

The funds, held by the State Bank of Pakistan, were initially for balance of payment support. While Pakistan can meet this repayment, it may require further external financing for upcoming fiscal needs.

articles.title: Men's heart risk starts earlier than you think
articles.summary: New research shows that men's risk of heart disease increases significantly by their mid-30s, much earlier than previously thought. This risk pattern differs from women's, even after accounting for lifestyle factors. The findings suggest the need for earlier screening programs to address this health disparity. The study was reported by TOI India.
articles.raw_summary: New research reveals men's heart disease risk escalates significantly by their mid-thirties, years before symptoms manifest. This divergence from women's risk patterns, even after accounting for lifestyle factors, highlights a critical need for earlier screening.
